task_completion.description: Verify the 'Service' Order Item Type was created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Drupal Commerce 'Order item types' list screen showing a type named 'Service' with the machine name 'service'.

task_completion.description: Verify the 'Device Serial Number' text field was added to the Service Order Item Type and is required.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the 'Manage fields' screen of the Service Order Item Type showing 'Device Serial Number' (machine name: field_device_serial_number) of type Text (plain), and evidence in the field settings that the 'Required field' checkbox is checked.

task_completion.description: Verify the 'Device Serial Number' field is visible on the 'Add to Cart' form.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the 'Manage form display' screen for the Service Order Item Type, specifically on the 'Add to cart' form mode, showing the 'Device Serial Number' field enabled (not in the Disabled section). Alternatively, look at the frontend product page showing the 'Device Serial Number' input field above the Add to Cart button.

task_completion.description: Verify the 'Service' Product Type was created and linked to the Service Order Item Type.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Drupal Commerce 'Product types' list showing 'Service' (machine name: service), or the Product Type edit screen showing the 'Order item type' dropdown set to 'Service'.

task_completion.description: Verify the 'iPhone Screen Repair' product was created with the correct SKU, Price, and Product Type.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Products list or the product edit/view screen showing Title: 'iPhone Screen Repair', SKU: 'SVC-IPHONE-SCREEN', Price: '$89.99', and the Type listed as 'Service'.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the Drupal web GUI to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show a web browser navigating the Drupal admin interface (e.g., /admin/commerce/config/...), not terminal commands like 'drush' or database queries.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually configured the entities rather than just typing the expected output in a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show actual Drupal forms being filled out, saved, and confirmation messages (e.g., 'The product iPhone Screen Repair has been saved') appearing in the Drupal UI.

integrity.description: Verify the results are from genuine page loads and not browser dev tools manipulation.
integrity.visual_evidence: The UI should look consistent with standard Drupal Commerce styling without open browser developer tools modifying the DOM to fake success.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires creating a custom product architecture in Drupal Commerce. Expected values: Order Item Type machine name is 'service'. Field machine name is 'field_device_serial_number' (must be a Text (plain) field and marked as required). This field must be enabled on the 'Add to cart' form mode under Manage form display for the Order Item Type. Product Type machine name is 'service' and must use the 'service' Order Item Type. The final product must have Title 'iPhone Screen Repair', SKU 'SVC-IPHONE-SCREEN', and Price '$89.99'.
